target.name: Addionics
target.founder: Moshiel Biton, Farid Tariq, Vladimir Yufit

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $39M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Addionics is a Series B climate and energy technology company that has raised $39M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: KoBold Metals ($3.0B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), and Amogy ($700M, Series C). The broader comparable universe spans 9 private firms and 1 public company (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $39M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $74M. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against the three comparable companies listed above, which exhibit a median valuation of $1.0B with a range of $700M to $3.0B. The peer-median method implies $1.0B.

The triangulated analysis yields a range of $63M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $537M. The wide dispersion reflects tension between the company's early-stage funding history and the significantly higher valuations of Series C peers in the same sector. Key uncertainties include whether Addionics' technology and market traction justify peer-level valuations at an earlier stage, and whether the three-company peer set adequately reflects market segmentation within climate and energy subsectors.
